title: The Day The Earth Stood Cool

jokes.text: What a lucky little boy to get a trip to the park with his grandpa. Grandpa?!
jokes.setup: Woman flirts with Homer thinking he's attractive, calls Bart lucky to be with his grandpa
jokes.punchline: Homer's horrified reaction to being mistaken for a grandfather
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vanity setup with perfect timing. The economy is excellent - minimal setup for maximum payoff.

jokes.text: This boy's grandfather is a shriveled-up corncob begging for death!
jokes.setup: Woman realizes Homer is the father, not grandfather
jokes.punchline: Absurdly harsh description of Homer as decrepit
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd insult with vivid imagery. 'Shriveled-up corncob' is memorably specific and quotable.

jokes.text: Beat it, ducks! I'm not old enough to have food for you!
jokes.setup: Homer's crisis about being seen as old
jokes.punchline: Yelling at ducks as proof of youth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - the absurd connection between age and feeding ducks shows his panicked mindset.

jokes.text: This drawing doesn't think I'm old. Don't you, drawing?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to prove he's not old
jokes.punchline: Talking to a drawing for validation
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer delusion moment. Works but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Russian nesting donut!
jokes.setup: Description of elaborate donuts
jokes.punchline: Absurd concept of nested donuts like Russian dolls
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd concept that's immediately visual and memorable.

jokes.text: Actually, Carl, there's no workplace-appropriate use for the word 'bazooms.' What about 'thunderbags'?
jokes.setup: HR person corrects inappropriate language
jokes.punchline: Carl suggests an even more ridiculous alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where the 'correction' is even worse. 'Thunderbags' is hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: 911. What is your emergency? I'm calling to report a missing donut cart.
jokes.setup: Serious 911 emergency line
jokes.punchline: Homer treating missing donuts as an emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priorities joke. Predictable but executed well for character consistency.

jokes.text: But, Chief, the power lines! 'But, Chief, the power lines.' Go lower!
jokes.setup: Lou warns about dangerous power lines
jokes.punchline: Wiggum mocks the safety concern and orders them to continue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - dismissing legitimate safety concerns with mockery.

jokes.text: What have I become? It's not a good feeling when you're saying 'what have I become?' more than once a day.
jokes.setup: Homer's moment of self-reflection
jokes.punchline: Meta observation about having too many moments of self-reflection
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about self-awareness. The specificity of 'more than once a day' makes it relatable and quotable.

jokes.text: Beer keg donut?!
jokes.setup: Terence offers a sneak preview of a new donut
jokes.punchline: Visual of a donut shaped like a beer keg
jokes.explanation: Combines Homer's two favorite things. Visual concept is clear and fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: Do you know there are now restaurants there with two locations? Not near my kids.
jokes.setup: Terence complaining about Portland becoming commercialized
jokes.punchline: Homer's horror at the concept of chain restaurants
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of hipster authenticity obsession. Homer's naive agreement adds another layer.

jokes.text: You can't have kids. Look at you. You have a fun job, a skull ring-- and it's not Halloween-- and your belt is a seat belt!
jokes.setup: Homer's disbelief that cool Terence has kids
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly specific cool details
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with specific details. The parenthetical about Halloween is a nice touch.

jokes.text: In my house, we found human bones.
jokes.setup: Terence talks about house's architectural bones
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual mention of actual human bones
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy delivery. The casual tone makes the horrifying implication even funnier.

jokes.text: Really? I'm... cool?! Oh, can I man-hug you? Sure. I don't know how!
jokes.setup: Homer's excitement at being called cool
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know how to man-hug
jokes.explanation: Good Homer vulnerability moment. The progression from excitement to confusion is well-paced.

jokes.text: Oh, relax, kids. Just be yourselves. Oh, great, Marge, now that's in their heads!
jokes.setup: Marge gives typical parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Homer points out how that advice creates pressure
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about parenting paradox. Homer being the one to point this out adds irony.

jokes.text: Uh, Mexico Chewy, not Star Wars Chewie. Star Wars Chewie is just a man in a costume, so he's not scary.
jokes.setup: Kid explains his armadillo's name
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact dismissal of Chewbacca as not scary
jokes.explanation: Child's logic about fictional characters is endearingly specific and fits the hipster kid character.

jokes.text: Uh, this is, um... Ice Cream, Bungee Jump, and... Viral Video.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to make his kids sound cool by renaming them
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and random 'cool' names
jokes.explanation: Good desperation comedy. The progression from 'Ice Cream' to 'Viral Video' shows escalating panic.

jokes.text: I did, when I was uno.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if they have Uno card game
jokes.punchline: Kid makes pun about being one year old
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun. Shows the precocious nature of the hipster kid character.

jokes.text: Fine. You have a weird old version of something.
jokes.setup: Kid's detailed explanation about toy authenticity
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive summary
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of hipster pretension. Bart cuts through the BS with simple honesty.

jokes.text: Uh, TV? We don't own a TV. I didn't know that was an option.
jokes.setup: Emily proudly announces they don't have TV
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent confusion
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash moment. Bart's genuine confusion highlights the pretension.

jokes.text: 'Scientists prove cat heaven real, human heaven not!' That's so sad! Those are joke headlines. It's not a real newspaper, it's just satire. Oh... satire!
jokes.setup: Lisa reacts emotionally to fake newspaper headline
jokes.punchline: Her excited discovery of satire as a concept
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Lisa's intellectual curiosity and naivety about adult humor.

jokes.text: Hmm, that's not applesauce.
jokes.setup: Marge realizes Emily is breastfeeding in front of her
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Subtle reaction comedy. Marge's understated response to an awkward cultural moment.

jokes.text: It's just that Maggie's already... milked me today.
jokes.setup: Awkward breastfeeding conversation
jokes.punchline: Marge's unfortunate choice of words
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness humor. The word choice creates unintentional crude imagery.

jokes.text: No, I'm young-person cool bald! Not old-person sad bald.
jokes.setup: Kids react to Homer's shaved head
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate distinction between types of baldness
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy about male vanity. The specific categories of baldness ring true.

jokes.text: Guess I can't be your best friend anymore. To be honest, that comes as a tremendous relief.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically announces end of friendship
jokes.punchline: Flanders' honest relief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders honesty subverting Homer's drama. Great character interaction.

jokes.text: I didn't go to college. Every CD I have, I bought at a car wash. Black-and-white films make me angry.
jokes.setup: Homer admits he's not cool
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific uncool admissions
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with specific, relatable details. The car wash CD detail is particularly good.

jokes.text: I can't pronounce 'artisanal.'
jokes.setup: Continuation of Homer's uncool admissions
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about the trendy word itself
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about hipster vocabulary. The simplicity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I only know David Cross from the Chipmunks movie.
jokes.setup: Homer's continued uncool confessions
jokes.punchline: Knowing a respected comedian only from kids' movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference showing cultural disconnect. Specific and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Not only do I like Van Halen, but I think they keep getting better.
jokes.setup: Homer's music taste confession
jokes.punchline: Genuinely controversial opinion about Van Halen's later work
jokes.explanation: Good music nerd humor. The progression implies he likes the heavily criticized later albums.

jokes.text: At the art walk, we walked into a gallery, and it was just a guy crying. That was the art!
jokes.setup: Lisa describing pretentious art experience
jokes.punchline: Performance art reduced to its most basic emotional element
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of pretentious performance art that captures the absurdity perfectly

jokes.text: Parenting shouldn't have a style! Parenting is about bedtimes, and passwords on computers, and complicated punishments you never follow through on!
jokes.setup: Marge objects to parenting as lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific list of practical parenting reality
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy about real parenting vs. trendy parenting. The final detail about unfollowed punishments is perfectly specific.

jokes.text: Wearing scarves in non-scarf weather is the essence of cool. The essence!
jokes.setup: Marge's criticism of his scarf
jokes.punchline: Homer's passionate defense of impractical fashion
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided certainty about fashion rules. The repetition of 'essence' adds emphasis.

jokes.text: And these skinny jeans will have flattened my junk for nothing!
jokes.setup: Homer's commitment to looking cool
jokes.punchline: TMI about physical discomfort from fashion
jokes.explanation: Standard TMI humor. The sacrifice for fashion angle works for the character.

jokes.text: T-Rex loves decaying industrial-scapes. I used to. Now I'm into abandoned ethnic union halls.
jokes.setup: Terence explains the venue choice
jokes.punchline: Even more pretentious evolution of tastes
jokes.explanation: Perfect hipster evolution - always moving to more obscure interests. The specificity is spot-on.

jokes.text: Glug, glug, glug. That's a good little mammal.
jokes.setup: Marge pretending to breastfeed with bottle
jokes.punchline: Awkward fake nursing sounds and dehumanizing language
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Marge's discomfort. The 'mammal' terminology adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Fine, I admit it, you nipple Nazis.
jokes.setup: Breastfeeding pressure from other moms
jokes.punchline: Marge's frustrated outburst with inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Bold line showing Marge's breaking point. The alliteration makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: If you don't want to breast-feed your baby, we will.
jokes.setup: Marge's admission about using formula
jokes.punchline: Creepy offer to nurse someone else's baby
jokes.explanation: Genuinely disturbing escalation of parenting judgment. Dark but effective comedy.

jokes.text: This is so pathetic. I can't even wear it ironically ironically.
jokes.setup: T-Rex receives Homer's handmade gift
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on levels of ironic appreciation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on hipster irony culture. The double ironic is perfectly pretentious and specific.

jokes.text: My vinyl. My vinyl!
jokes.setup: Fight breaks out at party
jokes.punchline: Hipster dad's priorities during chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect character priorities - caring more about records than children's safety. Repetition adds desperation.

jokes.text: I thought it made blimp tires. Yeah, like factories are never repurposed.
jokes.setup: Homer's confusion about factory history
jokes.punchline: Terence's condescending correction
jokes.explanation: Shows Terence's condescending nature. The sarcasm about factory repurposing is believably pretentious.

jokes.text: What? No one judges my wife's thunderbags.
jokes.setup: Homer learns about breastfeeding judgment
jokes.punchline: Defensive use of Carl's earlier inappropriate term
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Homer's protective instincts despite using inappropriate language.

jokes.text: They can 'goatee' hell.
jokes.setup: Homer's anger at the hipster neighbors
jokes.punchline: Pun combining 'go to' with facial hair reference
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that fits the hipster context. Homer's attempt at clever wordplay is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I like him-- he talks in rhyme and owns a whimsical store in a failing mall. He's like the dad in a Wes Anderson movie.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Flanders
jokes.punchline: Hipster appreciation for Flanders' quirky qualities
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about how Flanders would appeal to hipster aesthetics. The Wes Anderson comparison is perfect.

jokes.text: Parents are supposed to be lame so their kids have something to rebel against by becoming cool just long enough to get married and have kids for whom they are lame. It's nature's way.
jokes.setup: Argument about parenting philosophies
jokes.punchline: Homer's surprisingly insightful theory about generational cycles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical observation that's both funny and genuinely insightful. Homer's rare moment of wisdom is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: When you're old, your tattoos will be saggy and illegible.
jokes.setup: Homer's continued argument with hipster parents
jokes.punchline: Practical future reality of trendy body art
jokes.explanation: Good practical observation about aging and fashion choices. The visual imagery is effective.

jokes.text: When I humble-brag about this article, this towns about to get as popular as toe sneakers.
jokes.setup: Emily mentions their house being featured in a magazine
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of humble bragging and cultural prediction
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about social media behavior and trend cycles. The toe sneakers reference dates it perfectly.

jokes.text: I don't know-- everything crappy about this place, they like. Even the rats.
jokes.setup: Moe explains hipster takeover
jokes.punchline: Extension to literally everything, including vermin
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the gentrification theme. The rats detail pushes the concept to its logical extreme.

jokes.text: Disco Stu has found a new thing.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu appears in hipster context
jokes.punchline: Character known for being stuck in past adapts to new trend
jokes.explanation: Perfect character evolution joke. Shows how even the most dated character can find new trends to embrace.

jokes.text: I don't get a lot of sleep. I was out really late at an all-ages burlesque show.
jokes.setup: T-Rex explains why he thinks everything is lame
jokes.punchline: Child casually mentions inappropriate late-night entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about hipster parenting extremes. The casual tone makes the inappropriate content funnier.

jokes.text: Like those eels that eat whale poop?
jokes.setup: Homer asks to live on fringes of cool world
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and gross biological comparison
jokes.explanation: Homer's bizarre biological knowledge creates perfect metaphor for his social position. Gross but memorable.

jokes.text: 50 gallons of baby formula will put out that blaze.
jokes.setup: Need to fight fire with available materials
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous quantity of formula as fire suppressant
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes the earlier formula judgment payoff. Absurd but logical in context.

jokes.text: They're necessary. They're necessary! They're finally necessary!
jokes.setup: Wallet chains help form safety net
jokes.punchline: Homer's vindication about ridiculed fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for earlier fashion mockery. The escalating desperation in his voice works well.

jokes.text: That means Springfield is played out.
jokes.setup: New York Times names Springfield coolest city
jokes.punchline: Hipster logic that mainstream recognition ruins authenticity
jokes.explanation: Perfect hipster mentality - success equals selling out. Captures the impossible-to-please nature of trend culture.

jokes.text: Nuclear energy was a craft before it was a science. When uranium came from a stream, and atoms were split by hand, then transformed into the kind of electricity that illuminated not just our homes... but our souls as well.
jokes.setup: Homer gives pretentious speech about nuclear power
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated romantic history of nuclear technology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of artisanal/craft movement applied to nuclear power. The poetic language about atoms being 'split by hand' is absurdly beautiful.

tmdb_overview: A "cool family" from Portland moves in next door to the Simpsons. Homer is desperate to befriend them, while Marge is skeptical, thinking that being cool is not all it's cracked up to be.
